files.dependency_boundary: Public audit-domain constants and canonical model primitives define strict compliance-map records; built-in AICPA TSC, EU CRA, and ISO/IEC 27001 templates are consumed by the OSCAL exporter, CLI, documentation, and CRA pack source binding. No evidence collection, conformity adjudication, network retrieval, or external-standard content is owned here.
files.reassess_when: The file exceeds 950 lines, another built-in standard adds an independently versioned mapping lifecycle, template data moves beyond static declarations, or evidence verification is introduced.
files.responsibility: Versioned compliance-map protocol and built-in template owner for strict standard identities, supporting/partial/context relations, explicit unsupported domains, deterministic digests, non-certification language, and complete audit-domain coverage.

files.dependency_boundary: CRA registration, event, payload, submission, advisory, timeline-stage, Git-safe-read, and ignored internal-storage primitives define the persisted records and filesystem boundary. CLI argument parsing, network transport, authority submission, legal sufficiency, and conformity adjudication remain outside this owner.
files.reassess_when: The file exceeds 900 lines, advisory and Article 14 record replay become independently versioned storage protocols, another persistence backend is introduced, or filesystem custody separates from cross-record validation.
files.responsibility: Offline CRA append-only repository owner for bootstrap, registrations, event revisions, prepared payloads, receipts, skips, user notices, fixed-vulnerability advisories, content-addressed evidence replay, and fail-closed record-chain integrity.

files.dependency_boundary: Canonical audit primitives, GitInventory, GitRunner, and descriptor-based stable-read helpers provide identity and Git-ignore checks; scanner, campaign, CRA, and policy owners consume the resulting declarations and evidence. No rule verdict, CRA interpretation, or ignored-content persistence is owned here.
files.reassess_when: The file exceeds 850 lines, directory manifests gain an independently versioned public protocol, another capture family introduces a separate resource model, or descriptor traversal becomes a reusable storage subsystem.
files.responsibility: Policy-declared ignored-inventory protocol and collector owner for strict declarations, canonical evidence parsing and digests, bounded no-follow file and directory capture, resource limits, and concurrent filesystem-mutation rejection.

files.dependency_boundary: Public built-in adapter-profile, adapter-runtime, Git inventory, campaign, and candidate contracts; real Git repositories, executable fixtures, offline vulnerability databases, and sandbox provenance exercise Semgrep, Trivy, and OSV integration without reaching CLI or remediation authority.
files.reassess_when: The file exceeds 1100 lines, one adapter family adds an independent lifecycle or storage protocol, shared executable/database fixture construction moves beyond this owner, or a fourth built-in profile is introduced.
files.responsibility: Built-in adapter profile integration owner for availability, version and evidence parsing, scoped execution, offline database provenance, sandboxing, truncation and timeout failure, and campaign binding across Semgrep, Trivy, and OSV-Scanner.

files.dependency_boundary: Public ControlAssessment, EvidenceReference, ReviewerAttestation, EffectiveProfileLock, EffectiveControl, project-profile, and standard-pack records; trust-domain and real signing fixtures construct verified inputs. No CLI, campaign, filesystem, or remediation execution.
files.reassess_when: The file exceeds 950 lines, evidence or reviewer records gain a lifecycle independent of assessment acceptance, another protocol owner is added, or its builders become shared by another test module.
files.responsibility: ControlAssessment aggregate protocol owner for assessment states, nested evidence acceptance, independent reviewer acceptance, risk waivers, strict parsing, identity binding, and round trips.

files.dependency_boundary: Public CRA repository, product-registration, security-event, prepared-payload, submission-receipt, stage-skip, user-notice, and fixed-vulnerability advisory records; real temporary Git repositories exercise the ignored local store and append-only replay boundary. No CLI parsing, network transport, remote publication or submission, or conformity adjudication is exercised.
files.reassess_when: The file exceeds 1000 lines, advisory and Article 14 record replay require independent fixture lifecycles, another persistence backend is introduced, or its Git/store fixtures become shared by another test module.
files.responsibility: CRA append-only repository protocol owner for bootstrap and open, event revision chains, prepared drafts, receipts, stage skips, user notices, fixed-vulnerability advisories, crash replay, content-addressed paths, cross-record binding, and fail-closed tamper, link, ambiguity, and partial-state rejection.

files.dependency_boundary: Public content-addressed protocol constructors for inventory, Git provenance, policy, maturity, external sources, reports and report differences, reviews, campaigns, attestations, comparisons, adapters, StandardPacks, effective profiles, tasks, events, and closures. Normative graph schema/topology validation is owned separately by test_digest_dependency_graph.py; source-provenance, adapter-profile, and offline-verification mutation construction remain in their dedicated owners. This test directly uses repository_audit_git_repository, signing_fixtures, test_effective_profile, and test_work_models fixtures.
files.reassess_when: The file exceeds 900 lines, the normative graph partitions into independently versioned registries, a node family cannot remain mutation-tested through its separate owner, or another test-owner fixture dependency is introduced.
files.responsibility: Cross-protocol digest-mutation owner proving exact propagation, stable non-edges, canonical identities, and runtime graph queries over established production records.

files.dependency_boundary: Public ignored-inventory declarations, evidence, digest, scanner, policy, and real-Git test repository fixtures exercise descriptor and Git-ignore behavior. Monkeypatched filesystem primitives create deterministic replacement, mutation, hardlink, non-UTF-8, inaccessible, and resource-limit cases without reaching CRA rule semantics or external storage.
files.reassess_when: The file exceeds 1150 lines, directory-manifest and single-file capture require independent fixture lifecycles, another operating-system implementation is added, or race-fixture construction becomes shared test support.
files.responsibility: Ignored-inventory protocol and real-filesystem integration test owner for strict parsing, canonical evidence, file and directory capture, no-follow path custody, hardlink and special-file rejection, bounded resources, and deterministic concurrent-mutation failure.

files.dependency_boundary: Public remediation-plan types consume ControlAssessment, EffectiveProfileLock, project-profile, standard-pack, and canonical-digest records plus signing fixtures. Command-bound AdapterLock fixtures derive their digest through the public canonical_digest contract; no private production helper, remediation executor, or mutation authority is exercised.
files.reassess_when: The file exceeds 850 lines, execution authority is introduced, planning and scheduling become independently versioned protocols, or any additional private production helper is required by the tests.
files.responsibility: Advisory RemediationPlan protocol owner for gaps, steps, lanes, approval, dependency DAGs, conflict-safe batching, command binding, rollback bounds, and cross-record identity rejection.
